__fixture__.name: collector_backpressure_nonblocking
__fixture__.scenario: The observability_collector restarts after an internal OOM; the supervisor restarts it with exponential-capped backoff. Emitters (shell, supervisor, workers, helpers) keep emitting to bounded buffers while the collector is warming. The forensic packet for the collector's own restart is emitted immediately on respawn; the summary confirms that back-pressure dropped bounded data rather than serializing behind the collector. Support-bundle export assembly state is revoked; pending bundles are reissued.

heartbeat_snapshot.note: Collector heartbeated up to the moment of allocation failure.

resource_snapshot.note: Collector maintenance lane was at the constrained band; peak resident memory reached its hard cap before allocation failure.

capability_envelope_snapshot.note: Collector ingests typed streams; it does not hold subscription envelopes.

contract_versions.note: Audit-event-stream and typed RPC contracts current.

last_good_checkpoint.note: Retention-window checkpoint preserved on disk; bounded buffers resume from here on restart.

degraded_state_lineage.note: Collector respawned; emitters continue to write to bounded buffers.

revoked_tickets.note: In-flight support-bundle assembly revoked; any pending export is reissued after collector reaches ready.

emergency_action_linkage.note: No emergency action; internal allocation failure.

workspace_scope.note: Collector is ambient; not bound to a workspace, profile, or execution context.

summary: Observability collector crashed on allocation failure and respawned with a preserved retention-window checkpoint; emitters continued to fill bounded buffers without serializing behind the collector, satisfying the collector_is_non_blocking invariant.
